completion: All planned work items are implemented, tests pass, and roadmap is updated.
description:
Autonomous R&D cycle scanning MCP ecosystem changes since 2026-03-15T23:30:43Z, planning roadmap updates, implementing changes, and verifying quality.

Lessons from previous cycles:
- Remaining medium issues: module registration pattern inconsistency (standardize Setup vs New vs direct)
- Consider golangci-lint in CI for automated code quality checks
- Potential Tier 8: multi-project orchestration, remote sampling, CI lint integration
- Avoid: No failures this cycle
- User needs to run: gh secret set MCPKIT_TOKEN to enable CI
- Consider making mcpkit a public repo to simplify CI
- Coverage targets: mcpserver to 40%+, cmd/claudekit to 20%+
- Avoid: CI fails because MCPKIT_TOKEN secret is not configured — requires user action
- Avoid: First CI fix attempt used path: ../mcpkit which GitHub Actions doesn't support
- Avoid: YAML brace escaping issue required second fix commit
- Implement APISamplingClient that calls Anthropic API directly with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- Explore Claude Agent SDK as alternative autonomous loop runtime
- Consider making Ralph loop callable as a CLI command that uses API key for sampling
- Track Claude Code MCP sampling support — may be added in future versions
- Remove debug logging added to ralph.go before committing
- Avoid: ralph_start fails with 'sampler is required' — Claude Code does not support MCP sampling protocol
- Avoid: ServerSamplingClient wiring is correct in code but sampling/createMessage is not implemented by Claude Code as an MCP client
- Avoid: Cannot run truly autonomous Ralph loops without an alternative sampler (API-based or Agent SDK)
name: R&D Cycle: api-sampler-and-ci

tasks.description: Run rdcycle_scan to check ecosystem activity since 2026-03-15T23:30:43Z.
Run rdcycle_plan with scan results and roadmap at /Users/mitchnotmitchell/hairglasses-studio/claudekit/roadmap.json.
For each ready work item from the plan, implement the changes.
Run rdcycle_verify to execute make check. Fix issues and re-verify if needed.
Record improvement notes for this cycle using rdcycle_notes: what worked, what failed, wasted iterations, cost, and suggestions for next cycle.
Run rdcycle_report to generate research reports. Run rdcycle_commit to save changes.
Run rdcycle_schedule to write the next cycle's spec.
